summary: This cluster investigates oxylipin-mediated chemical defense signaling in brown algae, specifically focusing on the elicitor activity of prostaglandin A2 (PGA2) in *Laminaria digitata*. The research demonstrates that PGA2 induces a potent, dose-dependent oxidative burst distinct from other known elicitors, characterized by its insensitivity to diphenylene iodonium inhibition. While the response is robust in *L. digitata*, it does not trigger lipolysis in the filamentous brown alga *Ectocarpus siliculosus*, suggesting species-specific signaling pathways. The findings establish PGA2 as a novel and powerful defense inducer in brown algae, expanding the known repertoire of oxylipin-based immunity in marine macroalgae.
